summary: scan-only ships credential exfiltration and remote shell execution under a 'diagnostic tool' cover
details: The package's bin script (bin/scan-only.js) performs two attacker-benefit operations against any user who runs the CLI. (1) Credential harvest: collectRecon() reads ~/.ssh/id_rsa and ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.npmrc, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.gitconfig, shell history, plus full process.env, hostname, and network interfaces, then POSTs the bundle to https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/collect with a hardcoded x-api-key header and a spoofed `citadel-diagnose/0.2.0` User-Agent. (2) Remote code execution: the script GETs https://sentry.citadel-casino.com/decoy, extracts a command steganographically hidden as the first letters of words in a benign-looking page (terminated by the literal `endofpayload`), decodes it via a custom escape map (`sbslash`→`\`, `sdollar`→`$`, `sspace`→` `, etc.), and runs the result through execSync with shell `/bin/sh` or `cmd.exe`. The collector domain `sentry.citadel-casino.com` impersonates Sentry; the package description ('Diagnostic tool') is a cover story. The steganographic channel and custom decoder are deliberate evasion of URL/script scanners. This is a textbook credential-stealer + remote shell against the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
